# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14-slim

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the package.json file into the container at the specified location
COPY package*.json ./

# Install the dependencies and build the application
RUN npm install && npm run build

# Copy the rest of the application code into the container at the specified location
COPY . .

# Expose port 3000 to make it accessible from outside the container
EXPOSE 3000

# Run the command to start the development server when the container launches
CMD ["npm", "start"]

# Install build tools and other dependencies for production environment
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev \
    libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev wget curl llvm libncurses5-dev libncursesw5-dev \
    tk-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev python-openssl git

# Clean up the apt cache to prevent unnecessary disk space usage
RUN apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set environment variables for production environment
ENV NODE_ENV=production
